subject: Re: [PATCH 1/2] mm: vmalloc: streamline vmalloc memory accounting

reviews.author: Uladzislau Rezki
reviews.summary: reviewer suggested moving *_node_page_stat() calls to a single location in vm_area_alloc_pages(), specifically at the end or before splitting high-order pages, to avoid unnecessary looping over small pages
reviews.raw_body:
Can we move *_node_page_stat() to the end of the vm_area_alloc_pages()?

Or mod_node_page_state in first place should be invoked on high-order
page before split(to avoid of looping over small pages afterword)?

I mean it would be good to place to the one solid place. If it is possible
of course.

--
Uladzislau Rezk

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a mistake in their patch compilation and apologized for it.
reviews.raw_body:
Good catch, my apologies. Serves me right for not compiling
incrementally.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about memory accounting in the fallback paths, specifically when allocating pages from different nodes. They propose accumulating node counts until they differ and then flushing them, but are unsure if this is worth the overhead.

reviews.author: Uladzislau Rezki
reviews.summary: Reviewer agreed to keep the original solution and explicitly gave a Reviewed-by tag, indicating they have reviewed the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
I see. I agree it is easier to keep original solution. I see that
Andrew took it, but just in case:

Reviewed-by: Uladzislau Rezki (Sony) <urezki@gmail.com>

--
Uladzislau Rezki
